headline: HDFC Bank down ~1% on NIM pressure & chairman-exit fallout ahead of Jul 18 Q1 results
gist: HDFC Bank (6.6% Nifty weight) trading as a laggard, down ~1%, on NIM pressure from costlier deposit mix and lingering commentary around former chairman Atanu Chakraborty's abrupt exit, ahead of Q1 results due Jul 18.

headline: Axis Bank, Bajaj Finance, Shriram Finance rally >2%, driving Bank Nifty & Fin Services higher pre-earnings
gist: Axis Bank, Bajaj Finance and Shriram Finance all up more than 2% intraday, lifting Bank Nifty (+1.03%) and Nifty Financial Services (+1.29%) ahead of Axis Bank's Jul 18 results and on the back of Bajaj Finance's strong Q1 AUM print.

headline: Iran missiles hit tankers in Strait of Hormuz; US reinstates naval blockade on Iran
gist: Iran fired missiles at two ADNOC-operated tankers in the Strait of Hormuz; Trump reinstated a naval blockade on Iranian shipping after briefly floating a 20% transit toll. Brent surged near $86 (one-month high); WTI more modest at $79.8 (+0.6%) as much of the move was already priced in from prior sessions.

headline: Freeport LNG maintenance slashes feedgas; Henry Hub at 2-month low despite firm cooling demand
gist: Freeport LNG's turnaround maintenance (started Jul 10, two of three trains offline) is cutting feedgas deliveries and trapping supply domestically, pushing Henry Hub to a two-month low (~$2.87-2.92) despite strong cooling demand from the July heatwave.

headline: Gold fades from Tuesday's CPI-rally high as Fed Sept hike odds near 50% on oil-driven inflation fear
gist: Gold rallied to ~$4,098 Tuesday on soft US CPI (3.5% y/y) easing rate-hike fears, but has since faded back toward $4,035 as Iran-driven oil strength revives inflation concern and Fed Sept-hike odds firm near 50%. Silver tracking the same pattern, down to ~$58.7.
